methodsAttempted.notes: Verified Utrecht University control through the official www.uu.nl domain, the Utrecht University page branding, and the main robots.txt served from www.uu.nl.

methodsAttempted.query: site:uu.nl/en "generative AI" Utrecht University policy students assessment
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl web search returned the official AI Policy student pages, AI index, conditions of use, broader education articles, and an official news item.
Firecrawl web search located the central AI Policy hub and official pages for students, teachers, employees, researchers, and the UU AI Ethical Code of Conduct.
Firecrawl web search located official employee and researcher AI policy pages with privacy, security, and data-protection language.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery was limited to Utrecht University controlled www.uu.nl pages. Direct HTTP fetches were used for every verified evidence page after robots.txt review; Firecrawl search and page extraction helped identify and read official AI Policy pages. Broader education articles, a news item, and a CAT support hub were recorded as rejected because they are not direct policy evidence for staged claims.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Conditions of use of AI tools by students

artifacts.claimText: Utrecht University publishes an institutional AI Policy that is endorsed by the Executive Board and University Council and frames Responsible AI across AI systems and tools, not only generative AI.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Utrecht University uses an AI index for students as a five-level scale for how AI may be used, with teachers able to choose levels for a course or specific assignments.
artifacts.normalizedValue: student AI index levels set by course or assignment

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Utrecht University tells students not to use a UU student email address for AI-tool verification or account creation and notes that teachers cannot force students to create an AI-tool account while UU lacks secure AI tools.
artifacts.normalizedValue: student AI accounts should not use UU email for external tools

artifacts.claimText: Utrecht University's teacher AI Policy guidance says AI use in education must be ethical and legally compliant while the university retains human-in-the-loop freedom of action.
artifacts.normalizedValue: teacher guidance emphasizes responsible legal human-in-the-loop use

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.normalizedValue: research GenAI input should avoid personal or sensitive data unless data protection law is respected

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: This institutional AI policy is being endorsed by the Executive Board and the University Council and will be shaped based on legal frameworks, UU frameworks and our academic values. At UU we use the term Responsible AI by which we mean the ethical and responsible application of AI systems and tools. This is not limited to Generative AI but applies to all forms of AI.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Utrecht University describes an institutional AI policy endorsed by its Executive Board and University Council and defines Responsible AI across AI systems and tools.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: In education, we are working with the AI index. That is a 5-level scale indicating to what extent or how you may use generative AI or, for example, AI for data analysis etc. The teacher can choose from the 5 levels of AI use within the course as a whole, but can also choose to select different levels for specific assignments.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The AI index is a five-level scale for AI use, and teachers can set levels for a whole course or specific assignments.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Conditions of use of AI tools by students
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Do not enter your UU student email-address for verification purposes or to create an account. Be aware that free actually means that you are paying with your data. A teacher cannot force you to create an account in an AI tool because we cannot yet offer secure AI tools from the UU.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: For students, Utrecht says not to use a UU student email address for external AI-tool accounts and says teachers cannot force AI-tool account creation while UU lacks secure AI tools.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Free student subscriptions to AI tools
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: However, the use of AI systems and tools must be ethical and legally compliant, while we as a university and as humans-in-the-loop retain our own freedom of action. We want to exploit opportunities of AI and mitigate the risks.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The teacher guidance frames AI use around ethical, legal, human-in-the-loop use while exploiting opportunities and mitigating risks.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Employees may not use UU email or SolisID to create an AI-tool account unless the university supplies the AI system or tool and considers it safe.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Do not use any personal or sensitive data as input when using GenAI tools, unless you are certain that the data protection law is respected. Many risks pertaining to GenAI are not immediately visible. In many cases, it is necessary to assess the impact of your use of GenAI on data protection and privacy.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: For researchers, Utrecht says not to use personal or sensitive data in GenAI tools unless data protection law is respected, and says privacy impacts may need assessment.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Tips on protecting privacy and data security

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow source-status claim directly supported by the official Utrecht University AI Policy page.
Approved because the official AI index page directly supports the five-level student AI-use scale and teacher-level choice statement.
Approved because the official student AI conditions page directly supports the UU email and forced-account statements.
Approved as a narrow teaching-guidance claim tied to the official teachers AI Policy page.
Approved because the official employee AI Policy page directly states the UU email/SolisID restriction and safe university-supplied exception.
Approved because the official researcher risks page directly supports the personal/sensitive data and data-protection-impact statement.

artifacts.title: Utrecht University AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged official-source crawl for Utrecht University found a central AI Policy page plus official student, teacher, employee, and researcher AI Policy pages on www.uu.nl. Six agent-reviewed claim candidates were kept narrow: central policy status, student AI index treatment, student AI account privacy conditions, teacher responsible-AI guidance, employee safe-account restrictions, and researcher personal-data guidance. Three broader official pages were rejected as support, article, or news pages rather than direct policy evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Only public official www.uu.nl pages were used; no login-gated or private Utrecht University sources were accessed.
Firecrawl extraction for the researcher risks page returned a security-verification placeholder, so that page's staged evidence is based on static HTTP fetch text.
Rejected official pages were not used for approved claims because they were broader support, article, or news/pilot pages rather than direct policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
